message:
test(setjmp): drop registry entry per-test to fix flake (bd-7v4oe)

Six setjmp_abi unit tests use the global JumpRegistry keyed by the
test's stack-frame address of `marker`. Sequential tests on the same
parallel-test worker reuse stack addresses, so the registry
accumulates entries from prior tests. Under high test parallelism
(--test-threads=16) this manifested as a flake where
longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests aborted
with errno=22 (EFAULT) instead of the expected errno=38 (ENOSYS) —
restore_env reading freshly-captured mem_bytes mismatched a stale
in-registry entry and bailed before reaching the deferred-ENOSYS
panic path.

Add a `RegistryEntryGuard` RAII wrapper that calls
`registry().lock().remove(&env_addr)` on Drop, used by all five
tests that capture an env. The sixth test
(restore_env_missing_context_returns_einval) explicitly pre-cleans
its target address since a stale prior entry would make its lookup
spuriously succeed.

Verified: 6/6 setjmp_abi unit tests pass single-pass; full abi
sweep with --test-threads=16 stress (12 iterations) pending.
